description: Compute and map the solvent accessibility of all lysine residues in ubiquitin using PDB:1UBQ. Enable solvent-accessible area calculations in PyMOL (e.g., using `dot_solvent`), compute the individual Solvent Accessible Surface Area (SASA) for all 7 lysine residues (K6, K11, K27, K29, K33, K48, K63), and generate a text report at ~/PyMOL_Data/ubiquitin_lysine_report.txt. The report must list each of these 7 lysines and its SASA value, sorted in descending order from highest SASA (most exposed) to lowest SASA (least exposed). Finally, create a 3D visualization showing the overall protein as a grey or white surface, with the 7 lysine residues displayed as distinctly colored spheres (e.g., red) protruding from the surface. Save a high-resolution, ray-traced image of this visualization to ~/PyMOL_Data/images/ubq_lysines.png.
